preamble: The shift from the rhotic [r] to the high-front vowel [i] (or palatal glide [j]) is a form of vocalization. It occurs in specific dialects where syllable-final rhotics lose their trill and consolidate into a palatal resonance.

examples.note: Syllable-final r (and l) systematically vocalizes into a palatal glide in the Cibao region.
